quote: we identified and characterized a novel secreted protein, the extracellular serine protease EspP, which is encoded by the large plasmid of enterohaemorrhagic Escherichia coli (EHEC) O157:H7. ... It can be grouped into the autotransporter protein family.
uniprot_note: SPATE serine-protease autotransporter; plasmid pO157-encoded (RefSeq NC_002128.1, locus pO157p78 in Sakai).
citation_quote: the extracellular serine protease EspP, which is encoded by the large plasmid of enterohaemorrhagic Escherichia coli (EHEC) O157:H7 ... It can be grouped into the autotransporter protein family.

quote: Neisseria meningitidis secretes a protease that specifically cleaves the hinge region of immunoglobulin A1 (IgA1)
uniprot_note: IgA1 protease, the founding classical autotransporter (PF03797; Pohlner 1987 Nature 10.1038/325458a0 defined the AT mechanism in N. gonorrhoeae). Locus NMB0700 in N. meningitidis MC58; primary_ref is the MC58-lineage characterization.
citation_quote: As a member of the autotransporter protein family, IgA1Ps maturation involves several autoproteolytic cleavage steps leading to secretion of the approximately 100 kDa protease domain

quote: Antigen 43 is a surface-displayed autotransporter protein that mediates bacterial self-association and pathogenicity.
uniprot_note: Antigen 43 (Ag43) classical self-associating autotransporter; locus b2000/flu in MG1655.
citation_quote: Antigen 43 is a surface-displayed autotransporter protein that mediates bacterial self-association and pathogenicity.

quote: We have identified and characterized a secreted protein, designated Pic ... The deduced amino acid sequence of Pic shows high homology to autotransporter proteins, particularly a subgroup termed the SPATEs (serine protease autotransporters of the Enterobacteriaceae).
uniprot_note: Pic SPATE serine-protease/mucinase autotransporter; locus EC042_4593 in EAEC 042 (also c0350 in UPEC CFT073).
citation_quote: We have identified and characterized a secreted protein, designated Pic, which is encoded on the chromosomes of enteroaggregative (EAEC) 042 and 2457T.

quote: The secreted autotransporter toxin (Sat) of uropathogenic Escherichia coli exhibits cytopathic activity ... Sat, a vacuolating cytotoxin expressed by uropathogenic E. coli CFT073
uniprot_note: Sat SPATE secreted autotransporter toxin; locus c3619 in CFT073.
citation_quote: Sat, a vacuolating cytotoxin expressed by uropathogenicCFT073, elicits defined damage to kidney epithelium during upper urinary tract infection

organism: Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B str. MC58
quote: NadA has a predicted molecular structure strikingly similar to a novel class of adhesins (YadA and UspA2), forms high molecular weight oligomers, and binds to epithelial cells in vitro
uniprot_note: NadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (YadA-like, PF03895); locus NMB1994 in MC58.
citation_quote: NadA has a predicted molecular structure strikingly similar to a novel class of adhesins (YadA and UspA2), forms high molecular weight oligomers, and binds to epithelial cells in vitro

quote: The non-fimbrial adhesins, YadA of enteropathogenic Yersinia species, and UspA1 and UspA2 of Moraxella catarrhalis ... reveal a novel class of adhesins ... a tripartite organization: an N-terminal oval head domain ... a putative coiled-coil rod and ... a C-terminal membrane anchor domain.
uniprot_note: YadA trimeric autotransporter adhesin (prototype TAA); virulence plasmid pYVe8081 (RefSeq NC_008791.1), locus YEP0066.
citation_quote: an N-terminal oval head domain is followed by a putative coiled-coil rod and terminated by a C-terminal membrane anchor domain.

quote: We discovered a novel secreted protein by Pseudomonas aeruginosa, PlpD ... The association between a TpsB component and a passenger protein is a unique hybrid organization that we propose to classify as Type Vd.
uniprot_note: PlpD patatin-like hybrid autotransporter, T5dSS type example; locus PA3339 in PAO1.

quote: we conclude that proteins of the intimin and invasin family constitute a novel group of autotransported proteins, and propose that this class of autotransporters be termed Type Ve secretion.
uniprot_note: Intimin (EaeA), inverse autotransporter T5eSS; locus ECs4559 (Sakai) / Z5110 (EDL933).
citation_quote: the N-terminal β-barrel domain acts as a translocation pore to secrete the C-terminal passenger domain... we propose that this class of autotransporters be termed Type Ve secretion.

quote: Two-partner secretion (TPS) systems use beta-barrel proteins of the Omp85-TpsB superfamily to transport large exoproteins across the outer membranes of Gram-negative bacteria. The Bordetella FHA/FhaC proteins are prototypical of TPS systems in which the exoprotein contains a large C-terminal prodomain that is removed during translocation.
uniprot_note: FhaB (filamentous hemagglutinin, FHA) is the TpsA exoprotein; cognate TpsB transporter is FhaC (locus_tag BP1884, UniProt P35077). BP1879 is the old_locus_tag in the Tohama I (BX470248) annotation; RefSeq protein WP_010930610.1.
citation_quote: FHA is initially synthesized as an ~375 kDa preproprotein called FhaB... TpsA family members include adhesins, such as filamentous hemagglutinin (FHA)

quote: The ShlA hemolysin of Serratia marcescens is secreted across the outer membrane by the ShlB protein; ShlB belongs to the two-partner secretion system (type Vb), a subfamily of the Omp85 outer membrane protein assembly and secretion superfamily.
uniprot_note: TpsB partner is ShlB (locus_tag SMDB11_RS18525 / old SMDB11_3736, ShlB/FhaC/HecB family hemolysin secretion protein); shlBA operon, the two genes are adjacent. SMDB11_RS18530 (old SMDB11_3737) is RefSeq-annotated generically as 'filamentous hemagglutinin N-terminal domain-containing protein' (the PF05860 TpsA/Haemagg_act signature), placed here as the genome-located ShlA-family TpsA in the lab reference strain Db11. The historical ShlA characterization (UniProt P15320) is from an earlier non-genome strain; primary_ref quote is the type-Vb framing for the Serratia ShlA/ShlB pair.
citation_quote: The ShlA hemolysin of Serratia marcescens is secreted across the outer membrane by the ShlB protein; ShlB belongs to the two-partner secretion system (type Vb)

quote: FhaC proved to be unable to mediate secretion and activation of HpmA, the cognate secretory partner of HpmB. In contrast, ShlB, the accessory protein of the closely related Serratia marcescens hemolysin, was able to activate and secrete HpmA.
uniprot_note: HpmA = calcium-independent hemolysin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HpmB (locus_tag PMI2056, UniProt B4F0Z8); hpmBA operon. PMI2057 is old_locus_tag; current RefSeq locus PMI_RS10140.
citation_quote: FhaC proved to be unable to mediate secretion and activation of HpmA, the cognate secretory partner of HpmB

quote: HxuA is secreted by its specific transporter HxuB. HxuA/HxuB belongs to the so-called two-partner secretion systems (TPSs) that are characterized by a conserved N-terminal domain in the secreted protein which is essential for secretion.
uniprot_note: HxuA = heme-haemopexin binding protein, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HxuB (hxuCBA locus; HxuB UniProt P45355). locus_tag HI_0264 in Rd KW20.
citation_quote: HxuA is secreted by its specific transporter HxuB. HxuA/HxuB belongs to the so-called two-partner secretion systems (TPSs) that are characterized by a conserved N-terminal domain in the secreted protein which is essential for secretion.

quote: Wild-type H. ducreyi strain 35000HP contains two genes, lspA1 and lspA2, whose encoded protein products are predicted to be 456 and 543 kDa, respectively ... These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of H. ducreyi are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen.
uniprot_note: LspA1 = Large supernatant protein 1, a filamentous-hemagglutinin-family (PF05860) TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is LspB (locus_tag HD_1155, UniProt G1UBA6). locus_tag HD_1505 confirmed (UniProt Q7VLE8). Quote is from the foundational lspA1/lspA2 paper; the TPS/TpsB framing for the Lsp system is established in Guerin 2017 (10.3389/fcimb.2017.00148).
citation_quote: These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of [H. ducreyi] are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen

quote: Wild-type H. ducreyi strain 35000HP contains two genes, lspA1 and lspA2, whose encoded protein products are predicted to be 456 and 543 kDa, respectively ... These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of H. ducreyi are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen.
uniprot_note: LspA2 = Large supernatant protein 2, a second TpsA exoprotein in H. ducreyi 35000HP sharing the LspB TpsB transporter. locus_tag HD_1156 confirmed via GenPept (protein gi 33148493, /gene=lspA2, AE017143.1:923760..938519); LspB is the immediately adjacent HD_1155. lspA1 (HD_1505) is the paralogous TpsA elsewhere on the chromosome.
citation_quote: These results indicate that the LspA1 and LspA2 proteins of [H. ducreyi] are involved, directly or indirectly, in the antiphagocytic activity of this pathogen

quote: Bacterial contact-dependent growth inhibition (CDI) is mediated by the CdiB/CdiA family of two-partner secretion proteins. CDI systems deploy a variety of distinct toxins, which are contained within the polymorphic C-terminal region (CdiA-CT) of CdiA proteins.
uniprot_note: CdiA = contact-dependent growth-inhibition toxin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is CdiB (cdiBAI operon, cdiB upstream of cdiA). locus_tag ECP_4580 in UPEC 536 (UniProt Q0T963).
citation_quote: CDI is mediated by the CdiB/CdiA family of two-partner secretion proteins ... The CdiA-CT from uropathogenic E. coli 536 (UPEC 536) is a tRNA anticodon nuclease

quote: Upon cell contact, the toxic C-terminus of the TpsA family member CdiA, called the CdiA-CT, inhibits the growth of CDI(-) bacteria ... A requirement for bcpO in function of BcpA (the TpsA family member) was demonstrated, indicating that bcpAIOB define a novel class of TPS system.
uniprot_note: BcpA = Burkholderia CdiA-class contact-dependent inhibition toxin, a TpsA-family exoprotein; bcpAIOB locus. TpsB partner is BcpB (the TpsB family member, downstream in bcpAIOB). locus_tag BTH_I2723 (old) = BTH_RS26080 (current RefSeq), chromosome I NC_007651.1.
citation_quote: They export large exoproteins (TpsA family members such as CdiA) across the outer membrane using pore-forming beta-barrel proteins (TpsB family members such as CdiB).

quote: The two-partner secretion pathway in Gram-negative bacteria consists of a TpsA exoprotein and a cognate TpsB outer membrane translocator protein ... we characterized the functional domains of the Haemophilus influenzae HMW1B protein, a TpsB protein that interacts with the H. influenzae HMW1 adhesin.
uniprot_note: HMW1A (HMW1) = high-molecular-weight adhesin, the TpsA exoprotein. TpsB partner is HMW1B (locus_tag R2846_0713, adjacent; hmw1ABC operon). locus_tag R2846_0714 in strain R2846 (= strain 12, the strain HMW1 was characterized in), chromosome NC_017452.1.
citation_quote: the periplasmic domain is required for secretion of HMW1 and that the C-terminal membrane anchor (HMW1B-(234-545)) is capable of oligomerization and pore formation.

quote: We have characterized one of these components, encoded by the PA4625 gene, as a putative adhesin and designated it cdrA. CdrA shares structural similarities to extracellular adhesins that belong to two-partner secretion systems. The cdrA gene is in a two gene operon that also encodes a putative outer membrane transporter, CdrB.
uniprot_note: CdrA = c-di-GMP-regulated biofilm-matrix adhesin, the TpsA exoprotein (secreted as a 220 kDa proprotein, processed to 150 kDa). TpsB partner is CdrB (cdrAB operon, cdrB = PA4624). locus_tag PA4625 in PAO1, chromosome NC_002516.2.
citation_quote: CdrA shares structural similarities to extracellular adhesins that belong to two-partner secretion systems. The cdrA gene is in a two gene operon that also encodes a putative outer membrane transporter, CdrB. ... processed to 150 kDa before secretion into the extracellular medium.
